rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the one substantive ground-truth concern (yenuo26's question about verifying reduce_scatter behavior beyond hasattr) — X even explicitly marks that exact test code as 'validated...correct' without flagging it, so recall is low for both. X offers five concrete, diff-grounded, file/line-anchored findings (test-order fragility, untested cfg=1 path, untested None/world_size<=1 branches, etc.) that are valid if minor, giving it stronger actionability; Y's deeper but narrower investigation yields only one vague 'worth a comment' nit and otherwise reads as a pure approval, matching real-world outcome but contributing little concrete, actionable content.
